rationale: The citing paper’s core constructs are processing fluency/disfluency and evaluation, and its own note says it “extends prior processing-fluency research,” which aligns closely with the cited paper’s central contribution on conceptual and perceptual fluency effects on brand evaluation. Given this strong conceptual overlap and an exact DOI-matched reference (10.1509/jmkr.41.2.151.28665), this citation is plausibly a substantive theoretical foundation rather than a casual mention.
